title: Advanced Architectural Modeling and Texturing
description:
Updated in May 2025.
 
This course now features Coursera Coach!
 
A smarter way to learn with interactive, real-time conversations that help you test your knowledge, challenge assumptions, and deepen your understanding as you progress through the course.

This course is designed to help you master advanced techniques in architectural modeling and texturing using Blender. You will learn to create detailed architectural elements like doorways, windows, and balconies, while also exploring the art of texturing to bring your models to life. By the end of this course, you will have the skills to design highly realistic and visually appealing 3D architectural scenes.

The course begins with an in-depth exploration of doorway design, including the importance of normals and advanced unwrapping techniques for wood textures. You’ll then learn how to add natural elements like foliage, pottery, and plant pots to enhance the aesthetic appeal of entrances. The course continues with advanced lessons on window design, including mirror modifiers, cloth physics for curtains, and techniques for fixing mesh gaps.

As you progress, you will delve into balcony design, where you’ll use geometry nodes to create detailed planks and support structures. Additionally, you will learn how to model elegant curved staircases and refine your window and door models with advanced texturing techniques. The course concludes with a focus on camera framing, rendering setup, and the application of stylized architectural elements, such as arches and decorative railings.

This course is ideal for intermediate to advanced Blender users who want to enhance their architectural design and texturing skills. A basic understanding of 3D modeling and Blender’s interface is required, and this course is suitable for those interested in creating high-quality architectural visualizations for professional portfolios or projects.
